'''Chris Smith''' (born 16 January 1994) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|sr}} forward and {{rlp|lk}} for the [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]].

He previously played for the [[Sydney Roosters]] and the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] in the [[National Rugby League]], and the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]] at representative level.

==Background==
Smith was born in [[Darwin, Northern Territory]], Australia.

He played his junior rugby league for the [[Nightcliff Dragons]] in the [[Northern Territory Rugby League|Darwin Rugby League]] and attended Casuarina High School, before being signed to a contract by the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] at age 15.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.leagueoftitans.com.au/showthread.php?15456-Cowboys-Sign-Nt-Junior-Talent|title=Cowboys Sign Nt Junior Talent|publisher=Leagueoftitans.com.au|access-date=2016-04-07}}</ref> At 17 years of age, he signed with the [[Penrith Panthers]], moving to [[Sydney]] and attending [[Patrician Brothers' College, Blacktown]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/gio-schoolboys-cup-patrician-brothers-defeat-farrer/tabid/10874/newsid/69287/default.aspx |title=GIO Schoolboys Cup: Patrician Brothers defeat Farrer |publisher=NRL.com |access-date=2017-03-23}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://websites.sportstg.com/assoc_page.cgi?client=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26426&&news_task=DETAIL&articleID=21436436 |title=2012 - England Academy - Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League |publisher=SportsTG  |date=2013-11-17 |access-date=2017-03-23 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170323233024/http://websites.sportstg.com/assoc_page.cgi?client=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26426&&news_task=DETAIL&articleID=21436436 |archive-date= 2017-03-23 }}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.ntnews.com.au/sport/nightcliff-dragons-to-penrith-panthers-chris-smith-to-make-nrl-debut-against-newcastle-knights/story-fnk0b28u-1227511858630|title=Nightcliff Dragon to make debut with Penrith Panthers|author=Wake, Rebekka|newspaper=NT News|date=3 September 2015|access-date= 5 September 2015}}{{dead link|date=January 2024}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
From 2012 to 2014, Smith played for the Penrith Panthers' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://nycdatabase.org/players/s/|title=S|publisher=Nyc Database|access-date=2015-09-05 |url-status=usurped |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160131072555/http://nycdatabase.org/players/s/ |archive-date= Jan 31, 2016 }}</ref> In August 2012, he played for the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsportspulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=1-2130-0-0-0&sID=26416&news_task=DETAIL&articleID=19907670|title=2012 Australian Team Announced – Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|publisher=Fox Sports Pulse |date=2012 |access-date=2015-09-05 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150924015334/http://www.foxsportspulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=1-2130-0-0-0&sID=26416&news_task=DETAIL&articleID=19907670 |archive-date= Sep 24, 2015 }}</ref>

===2015===
In 2015, Smith graduated to the Penrith [[New South Wales Cup]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.penrithpanthers.com.au/news/2015/05/05/round_9_team_list_0.html|title=Round 9 Team List |date=5 May 2015 |publisher=Penrith Panthers|access-date=2015-09-05 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150924071148/http://www.penrithpanthers.com.au/news/2015/05/05/round_9_team_list_0.html |archive-date= Sep 24, 2015 }}</ref> 
[[File:Chris Smith Penrith.jpg|thumb|right|Smith playing for the Panthers in 2015]]
In Round 26 of the [[2015 NRL season]], he made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for Penrith against the [[Newcastle Knights]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/updated-round-26-nrl-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/89403/default.aspx |date=6 Sep 2015 |title=Updated: Round 26 NRL team lists|publisher=NRL.com|access-date=2015-09-05}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2015/round-26/penrith-vs-newcastle/summary.html|title=NRL 2015 - Round 26 - Penrith Panthers 30 def. Newcastle Knights 12 |publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=2016-04-07 |url-status=live |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20230930022535/https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2015/round-26/penrith-vs-newcastle/summary.html |archive-date= Sep 30, 2023 }}</ref>

===2016===
On 6 April, Smith was released from his Penrith contract to join the [[Sydney Roosters]] effective immediately, on a contract to the end of 2017.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/panthers-release-chris-smith-21318/ |date=April 6, 2016  |title=Panthers release Chris Smith|publisher=Zero Tackle|access-date=2016-04-07}}</ref>

===2017===
Smith made only one first grade appearance for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[2017 NRL season]] which came against Canberra in round 12 and ended in a 16-24 loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2017/round-12/canberra-vs-sydney/summary.html/|title=NRL 2017 - Round 12 - Canberra Raiders 24 def. Sydney Roosters 16 |website=Rugby League Project |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230929235144/https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2017/round-12/canberra-vs-sydney/summary.html/ |archive-date= Sep 29, 2023 }}</ref>

===2018===
At the end of 2017, Smith signed a contract with [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury-Bankstown]].  Smith made only one appearance for Canterbury in the [[2018 NRL season]] which came against Brisbane in round 21 and ended in a 36-22 victory at [[ANZ Stadium]].  Smith spent most of the year playing with Canterbury's reserve grade team.  Smith played in the club's [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] grand final victory over Newtown at [[Leichhardt Oval]].  The following week, Smith played in Canterbury's [[NRL State Championship]] side which defeated Redcliffe 42-18 at [[ANZ Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/bulldogs/canterbury-bulldogs-beat-newtown-jets-to-win-2018-intrust-super-premiership-grand-final/news-story/5194d899167222fcbc817a6dfe96cf09/|title=Canterbury Bulldogs beat Newtown Jets to win 2018 Intrust Super Premiership grand final|website=Fox Sports |date=September 23, 2018 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230522231009/https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/bulldogs/canterbury-bulldogs-beat-newtown-jets-to-win-2018-intrust-super-premiership-grand-final/news-story/5194d899167222fcbc817a6dfe96cf09 |archive-date= May 22, 2023 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/09/30/bulldogs-defeat-redcliffe-dolphins-to-win-intrust-super-championship/|title=Bulldogs down Dolphins to win Intrust Super Championship|website=NRL |first1=Chris |last1=Kennedy |date=30 Sep 2018 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230406054638/https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/09/30/bulldogs-defeat-redcliffe-dolphins-to-win-intrust-super-championship/ |archive-date= Apr 6, 2023 }}</ref>

===2019===
Smith made a total of 20 appearances for Canterbury in the [[2019 NRL season]] as the club finished 12th on the table.  Smith only missed 5 matches all season and cemented himself in the first grade squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.brisbanetimes.com.au/sport/spoons-of-wood-high-hopes-and-chances-lost-the-final-round-storylines-20190905-p52o87.html/|title=Spoons of wood, high hopes and chances lost... the final round storylines|website=Brisbane Times |first1=Phil |last1=Lutton |date=September 5, 2019 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230711185831/https://www.brisbanetimes.com.au/sport/spoons-of-wood-high-hopes-and-chances-lost-the-final-round-storylines-20190905-p52o87.html |archive-date= Jul 11, 2023 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/09/01/the-dean-pay-way-unashamedly-old-school/|title=The Dean Pay way: 'Unashamedly' old school|website=NRL |first1= Margie |last1=McDonald |date=1 Sep 2019 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230711184827/https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/09/01/the-dean-pay-way-unashamedly-old-school |archive-date= Jul 11, 2023 }}</ref>
On 22 November 2019, Smith signed a two-year contract extension to stay at Canterbury until the end of the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/chris-smith-signs-new-deal-with-canterbury-bulldogs/|title=Chris Smith signs new deal with Canterbury Bulldogs|website=Love Rugby League |first1=Drew |last1=Darbyshire |date=22 Nov 2019 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191127214234/https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/chris-smith-signs-new-deal-with-canterbury-bulldogs/ |archive-date= Nov 27, 2019 }}</ref>

===2020===
In February, Smith was ruled out for the first three months of the [[2020 NRL season]] after suffering a torn MCL and a partial ACL tear during the pre-season [[Indigenous All-Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]] game.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2020-paul-gallen-vs-dean-pay-kieran-foran-canterbury-bulldogs-cronulla-sharks-rugby-league/news-story/02799014bf1efca15690bf26fd0cc019/|title='Don't pay overs': Paul Gallen hits back at Dean Pay over Kieran Foran salary cap relief|website=Fox Sports |date=February 26, 2020 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230627032611/https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2020-paul-gallen-vs-dean-pay-kieran-foran-canterbury-bulldogs-cronulla-sharks-rugby-league/news-story/02799014bf1efca15690bf26fd0cc019/ |archive-date= Jun 27, 2023 }}</ref>

===2021===
On 31 August, Smith was one of twelve players who were told by Canterbury that they would not be offered a contract for the 2022 season and would be released at seasons end.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-bulldogs-confirm-departing-players-dylan-napa-lachlan-lewis-will-hopoate-roster-contracts/news-story/0f92571846a3b360fa3df2cd52db5dd8/|title=Bulldogs show Origin star the door as Gus puts broom through Belmore|website=Fox Sports |date=August 31, 2021 |first1=Darcie |last1=McDonald |url-status=deviated |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210831082845/https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-bulldogs-confirm-departing-players-dylan-napa-lachlan-lewis-will-hopoate-roster-contracts/news-story/0f92571846a3b360fa3df2cd52db5dd8 |archive-date= Aug 31, 2021 }}</ref>
In November, Smith signed a contract to return to the [[Penrith Panthers]] for the 2022 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.penrithpanthers.com.au/news/2021/11/22/smith-signs-panthers-deal/|title=Smith signs Panthers deal|website=Penrith Panthers |date=22 Nov 2021 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230929235117/https://www.penrithpanthers.com.au/news/2021/11/22/smith-signs-panthers-deal/ |archive-date= Sep 29, 2023 }}</ref>

===2022===
Smith played six games for Penrith in the [[2022 NRL season]].  Smith played most of the year with the clubs [[NSW Cup]] team.  On 25 September, Smith played for Penrith in the clubs [[NSW Cup]] Grand Final victory over [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/draw/nsw-cup/2022/grand-final/game-1/|title=Panthers v Bulldogs - Grand Final, 2022 |website=NSWRL |access-date=4 October 2022|archive-date=29 September 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220929164236/https://www.nswrl.com.au/draw/nsw-cup/2022/grand-final/game-1/|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2022-10-03/penrith-panthers-win-all-four-grades-nrl-grand-final/101495282/|title=Penrith Panthers' back-to-back NRL premierships the culmination of grand final victories across all grades|website=ABC News |first1=Jon |last1=Healy |date=2 Oct 2022 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221207214341/https://www.abc.net.au/news/2022-10-03/penrith-panthers-win-all-four-grades-nrl-grand-final/101495282 |archive-date= Dec 7, 2022 }}</ref>
On 2 October, Smith played in Penrith's 44-10 victory over [[Norths Devils]] in the [[NRL State Championship]] final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2022/10/02/sunia-sizzles-as-panthers-down-devils-in-state-championship/|title=Sunia sizzles as Panthers down Devils in State Championship|website=www.nrl.com}}</ref>
